title: France will likely face third heatwave of the year this weekend and next week
subhead: Temperatures are set to drop tomorrow and Thursday across the country, before rising again Friday in the southern half. The duration, intensity and geographical extent of the episode remain uncertain at this stage.

Mbappé, Dembélé and Olise lead Deschamps' attacking revolution
subhead: With 10 goals scored, France ranked among the most efficient teams in the World Cup's group stage. The French manager will once again rely on his forwards against Sweden on Tuesday in the round of 32.

title: Iran prepares for late supreme leader's funeral
subhead: The funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed on February 28, the first day of the US-Iran war, was initially delayed but is now set to begin on Saturday. The ceremonies are expected to draw between 15 and 20 million mourners.

title: Serena beaten at Wimbledon in first singles match in four years
subhead: The legendary American tennis player Serena Williams, a seven-time Wimbledon champion, has made a sensational comeback at the age of 44. Though Serena lost her first round match to Australia's Maya Joint, her time at Wimbledon is not over: She is also playing in the doubles with her sister Venus.

title: 250 years of American independence: Sinclair Lewis, the Nobel Prize-winning author who 'burst the smugness' of the average American
subhead: 'America 250' (9/13). The 'Roaring Twenties' saw consumerism, puritanism, and xenophobia rise together in the United States. Sinclair Lewis, a native of Sauk Centre, a rural town in Minnesota, rose to fame with his biting satires of American life, which still resonate today.

'We should not accept that heatwaves cause thousands of deaths. That is not normal'
subhead: In an interview with Le Monde, Julien Dossier, founder of the environmental consulting firm Quattrolibri, offers an initial political assessment of how France responded to the most intense heatwave ever recorded in the country.

title: At the Four Seasons in Damascus, power shifts but the elite remain
subhead: A symbol of Bashar al-Assad's grip on Syria, the luxury hotel was the scene of lavish parties and business dealings until the fall of the dictator in December 2024. After briefly serving as the headquarters for the new leaders, it is now a networking hub for entrepreneurs seeking business opportunities in a country where everything must be rebuilt.

title: The killing of West Bank children by Israel's army, a product of Palestinians' dehumanization
subhead: The Israeli human rights organization B'Tselem documented the deaths of 54 minors killed by the Israeli army in the occupied West Bank in January 2025 and the impunity enjoyed by its soldiers.
